content.verdict: Kasbah Bab Ourika is a hilltop Berber-style eco-kasbah 45 minutes from Marrakech with one of the most astonishing views of the High Atlas you can have from a bed. Twenty-eight rooms, all oriented to catch the snow-capped peaks or the Ourika River in the valley below, a long infinity pool cut into the ridge, and a kitchen that leans entirely on the organic garden and the local Berber villages. For honeymooners who have already done Marrakech or are pairing city days with a mountain retreat, this is the most romantic mountain address in Morocco — quieter than a Medina riad, wilder than an Atlas resort, and with service that runs on genuine warmth rather than choreography.
content.best_room: Book a Premium Suite with terrace and direct Atlas view — the corner suites are worth the upgrade for the wraparound terrace and the in-room fireplace that gets lit every evening from October to April. If you want complete privacy, the Garden Suite with plunge pool offers a small walled garden and outdoor bath.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and Atlas sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private transfer from Marrakech (45 minutes) through the Ourika Valley, arrival mint tea on the terrace, golden-hour swim in the infinity pool as the Atlas turns pink, candlelit Berber dinner in the main dining room.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Seven cascades hike
itinerary_7_nights.description: Guided walk to the Setti Fatma waterfalls, lunch at a riverside Berber restaurant, return by mid-afternoon, couples hammam with ghassoul clay and rose, early fireplace dinner in your suite.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Berber village immersion
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning mule trek through apple and walnut orchards to a traditional Berber village, tea in a local home, lunch back at the kasbah, slow afternoon by the pool, stargazing with a telescope on the ramparts after dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Marrakech day trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Private driver to the Medina for a guided half-day of souks, Bahia Palace and Saadian Tombs, lunch at Le Jardin, afternoon at Jardin Majorelle and the YSL Museum, back up the valley in time for a bath and a sunset cocktail.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow mountain day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Yoga in the garden at sunrise, long breakfast, reading in the library, afternoon cooking class with the chef on Berber tagines and m'semen bread, private dinner on your terrace under lanterns.

itinerary_7_nights.title: High Atlas drive to Oukaïmeden
itinerary_7_nights.description: Full-day private 4x4 up to Oukaïmeden (2,600m) for panoramic views across the High Atlas, picnic lunch on the pass, stop at an argan cooperative on the way down, dinner poolside with Berber musicians.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell morning and departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Slow breakfast on the terrace, last dip in the infinity pool, late check-out at 14:00, farewell mint tea with the owners, private transfer to Marrakech Menara airport (1 hour).

caveats: The kasbah is genuinely remote — the access road is a narrow, steep track and evening excursions into Marrakech are not practical; plan city days as day trips rather than dinner outings.
At roughly 1,100m altitude, evenings and nights can be cold from November to March; rooms have fireplaces and underfloor heating, but pack layers even in spring.
Eco-kasbah means generator-assisted solar power and local sourcing — everything works well, but do not expect the 24-hour room service or complex technology of a city Four Seasons.

faqs.question: How do we get there from Marrakech?
faqs.answer: The kasbah arranges private transfers from Marrakech Menara airport or the Medina. The drive is about 45 minutes to 1 hour each way; the last kilometre is a steep dirt track that a standard car handles fine in dry weather.

faqs.question: Is it suitable for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes. With only 28 rooms, private terraces, in-room fireplaces, hammam for two and Atlas views from every bed, it is one of the most romantic addresses in Morocco. Most guests are couples and the atmosphere is quiet and slow-paced.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, children are welcome, but the remote location, lack of a kids' club and Berber-dinner rhythm mean the guest mix skews heavily to couples and small groups of friends.

faqs.question: Can we combine this with Marrakech?
faqs.answer: Yes, and we recommend it. Three nights in a Medina riad followed by four nights at Kasbah Bab Ourika is a classic honeymoon split; the kasbah arranges all transfers and day trips into the city.

faqs.question: What is the food like?
faqs.answer: Contemporary Berber cooking based on the organic gardens and local villages — tagines, couscous, slow-cooked lamb, vegetable mezze, homemade breads and jams. The set dinner menu changes daily and alcohol is available.

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: April to June and September to early November are the prime months; winter (December–February) is magical for fireplace and snow-capped peak views but requires warm clothing. July and August can be hot in the valley but are cooler than central Marrakech.

Dear Kasbah Bab Ourika team,

We are planning our honeymoon and would love to stay with you for 7 nights (or a combination of Marrakech + kasbah) from [ARRIVAL DATE] to [DEPARTURE DATE]. Could you please confirm availability and your best honeymoon rate for a Premium Suite with Atlas view, and a quote for a Garden Suite with plunge pool as an upgrade?

We would also appreciate:
- Private transfer from Marrakech Menara airport (RAK)
- Breakfast and daily set dinner included, if available
- A couples hammam ritual and a private terrace dinner during our stay
- A guided Berber village walk and a mule trek
- Late check-out on our departure day
